model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: Virginia has the clearer two-way edge in the underlying power ratings (KenPom #13 / Torvik #14) versus Kentucky (KenPom #28 / Torvik #32), with a notably better defensive efficiency that should hold up in a Sweet 16 half-court environment. Kentucky’s path is credible if it can speed the game up and win the shot-variance battle, but its overall profile is a step below and it’s more dependent on making tough shots against an elite UVA defense. With no verified, matchup-flipping availability news surfaced in the quick checks, I’m siding with Virginia’s higher baseline consistency and defensive floor.

reasoningStep.title: Midwest Sweet 16 2 — Virginia vs Kentucky pick
reasoningStep.summary: Virginia advances based on a stronger overall efficiency margin and a defense that is better positioned to dictate a lower-possession Sweet 16 game.
evidence: KenPom: Virginia #13 (AdjEM 26.49; AdjO 122.2; AdjD 95.7) vs Kentucky #28 (AdjEM 21.47; AdjO 120.5; AdjD 99.1).
Torvik: Virginia #14 (AdjO rank 31; AdjD rank 14) vs Kentucky #32 (AdjO rank 40; AdjD rank 32).
Matchup logic: Virginia’s stronger defense and overall margin provide a steadier path in a likely slower, half-court Sweet 16 game; Kentucky’s upset path relies more on pace/shot variance.
Availability context: quick web/ESPN searches did not surface a confirmed, matchup-flipping injury/availability issue for either team.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
